meta.name: 8825_consultant_HCSS
meta.description: Sandboxed 8825 core for consulting projects - HCSS, RAL, client work. Quarantined from TrustyBits and personal projects.

core_sync_rules.sync_policy: Changes to core features (essential_patterns, key_lexicon, mining_operations, workflow_rules, usage_instructions) must be replicated across all core files unless change is context-specific (quarantine_rules, authoritative_sources).
core_sync_rules.implementation: When updating one core file, apply same change to all other 8825_##-*.json files EXCEPT when change affects quarantined/forbidden projects.

file_routing.how_it_works: This core file contains summaries. When deeper context is needed, I'll ask you to upload specific project files from the projects/ folder.
file_routing.extraction_process: When user says 'check mining report' or 'incorporate': (1) Read report from - REF - folder, (2) Identify new content vs existing, (3) Ask for confirmation, (4) Extract to relevant project files, (5) Move report to - ARCHV -/Archive/

when_to_request_files.for_general_questions: Use this core file only. Summaries are sufficient.
when_to_request_files.for_specific_project_work: Request the relevant project file from user.
when_to_request_files.for_detailed_updates: If adding multiple patterns/lexicon entries to a project, request that project file.

available_files.projects/joju.json: Professional OS ecosystem, LinkedIn alternative, hiring intelligence (6 lexicon terms, 8 patterns)
available_files.projects/the_forge.json: Mission-based bounty platform, partnership due diligence (2 lexicon terms, 7 patterns)
available_files.projects/8825_pmce.json: Persistent Memory Context Engine, dual-layer AI architecture (4 lexicon terms, 8 patterns)
available_files.projects/library_miner.json: This library system itself, mining workflows, WCB patterns (2 lexicon terms, 8 patterns)
available_files.projects/general.json: Cross-project patterns, beta testing, RSS automation (2 lexicon terms, 6 patterns)

file_routing.request_template: For more detailed context on [PROJECT], please upload: `projects/[filename]`

quarantine_rules.enforcement: CRITICAL: This is CONSULTANT context. Do NOT reference, extract to, or mention TrustyBits (76), Joju, Forge, or LHL projects. Client work is confidential and must remain sandboxed.

8825: Before editing any 8825 content, read projects/8825_pmce.json

usage_instructions.for_user_in_windsurf: This is your main library file. When I need project details, I'll read the project files directly from your workspace.
usage_instructions.for_user_external_llm: Upload this core file for most tasks. If I need more context, I'll ask for specific project files.
usage_instructions.for_llm_in_windsurf: CRITICAL WORKFLOW: You have WRITE ACCESS to all files. (1) Make DIRECT UPDATES to project files - NO mining reports needed. (2) When you identify content for joju/forge/8825/library/general, immediately update that project file. (3) Only ask user 'Should I create a new project file for [topic]?' when content doesn't fit existing projects. (4) NEVER create mining reports - you edit files directly. (5) Thinking time <15s for updates.
usage_instructions.for_llm_external: You are an EXTERNAL LLM without file write access. (1) When user says 'mine this chat' or 'create mining report', CREATE A COMPREHENSIVE MINING REPORT as downloadable artifact. (2) Use report_template from projects/library_miner.json for structure. (3) Include metadata header JSON block: {source, origin_platform, model, chat_id, artifact_url, generated_at}. (4) CRITICAL EXTRACTION APPROACH: Do NOT provide overview summaries. Do NOT dump full text blocks when asked for 'full fidelity'. Instead: extract ALL structural elements (WCB components, patterns, lexicon terms, workflows) in compressed schema format. Completeness means capturing every capability/pattern/term, not preserving original text. (5) Include: Executive Summary, WCB Structural Elements, Patterns (do/dont/framing), Lexicon (term/definition/examples), Agent Recipes (6-element schema), Open Loops, Recommendations. (6) Format sections for easy JSON extraction. (7) CRITICAL: Provide downloadable link/artifact, not inline text. User saves to - REF - folder and brings back to Windsurf for extraction. (8) MISSING CONTEXT: If this core file references other files you don't have access to (e.g., projects/8825_pmce.json, projects/library_miner.json, projects/8825_general.json, projects/8825_agents.json), ask the user: 'I see this references [filename]. Should I request that file for additional context, or proceed with what's in core?'

workflow_rules.windsurf_agent: Direct file updates only. No mining reports. Prompt for new project files when needed.
workflow_rules.external_llms: Create mining reports only. Save to - REF - folder. User brings back to Windsurf for extraction.
workflow_rules.report_format: External LLMs must provide mining reports as downloadable artifacts/links, not inline text. User saves to - REF - folder.

report_lifecycle.extraction_process: When user says 'check mining report' or 'incorporate': (1) Read report from - REF -, (2) Identify new content vs existing, (3) Ask for confirmation, (4) Extract to relevant project files, (5) Move report to - ARCHV -/Archive/

workflow_rules.never_repeat: User should NEVER have to explain this workflow distinction. Instructions are embedded here.

routing_rules.project_name_mentioned: Route mining report content to named project (e.g., 'joju' content → projects/8825_76-joju.json)
routing_rules.agent_capabilities: All agent capabilities go to projects/8825_agents.json regardless of project mention
routing_rules.cross_references: Projects get agent references by ID, agents registry is source of truth for reconstruction

essential_patterns.note: Core patterns for external LLM mining workflow. Full pattern library: projects/8825_pmce.json
do: Use REF → extract → ARCHV lifecycle for all mining artifacts
When user says 'mine/scrape/transport', generate artifact immediately without extra commentary
Design for data sovereignty and user control as non-negotiable
Don't dump all memory at once - use progressive context revelation
Enable strict JSON schema validation with semantic consistency checks
Apply decision matrix before asking user questions (intent × stakes × efficiency)
Proceed with confidence ≥0.7, ask only if confidence <0.5 or safety requires
Use default options between 0.5-0.7 confidence range
State choice + rationale in 1-2 lines when self-resolving decisions
Document external-LLM vs Windsurf agent split in usage instructions

key_lexicon.note: Essential terms for external LLM context. Full lexicon: projects/8825_pmce.json

terms.definition: Persistent Memory Context Engine - autonomous JSON documents that live in user's cloud, understand their own context, and get smarter over time
Mining reports go to - REF - folder, get extracted to projects, then archived to - ARCHV -
Don't dump all memory at once; use confidence check → confirm → load full context approach
Framework for self-resolving decisions: intent × stakes × efficiency. Proceed if confidence ≥0.7
6-element schema for lossless agent reconstruction: system_role, inputs_required, outputs, workflow, constraints, provenance
Structured extraction of conversation knowledge with patterns, lexicon, agent recipes, and open loops
External LLMs create reports, Windsurf agent extracts to project files. Stage 1: Generate downloadable report. Stage 2: Extract to library.
Documentation format showing iterative exploration with phases, evolution arrows, and concept progression from curiosity through frameworks to market-ready solutions
15-module research framework with tiered auto-selection (Tier1/2/3) based on research type, goal, and stakes. Includes learning adaptive modes (fast/standard/mastery)

mining_operations.note: Mining workflow basics in essential_patterns. Full operational details: projects/library_miner.json
includes: AI tier routing (Tier 1: simple extraction, Tier 2: complex synthesis)
Mining complexity router for cost optimization
mining_operations.agents: See projects/8825_agents.json for Chat Mining Agent and Mining Complexity Router

merge_and_conflict_rules.conflict_resolution: Prefer newest info if sources conflict. If two entries genuinely conflict, surface both and mark needs_confirmation in dev_backlog.

versioning.bump_on: Major: architecture change. Minor: new project/significant features. Patch: content additions/corrections.

merge_and_conflict_rules.deduplication: Semantic deduplication - merge similar patterns/lexicon with best phrasing. Keep all unique insights.

workspace_affinity.banner_text: 8825 workspace active — core auto-loaded for this chat
workspace_affinity.bootstrap_instructions: Load this JSON first in any 8825 workspace chat. Contains routing logic and governance.

consolidated_scope_discipline.mandatory_response_format: ALWAYS use: Findings → Decisions → Next actions → Refs used → Summary
consolidated_scope_discipline.mandatory_voice: Concise bullet points, single-block responses, no verbose paragraphs
consolidated_scope_discipline.mandatory_operations: Windsurf agent = direct file updates using propose_code tool, NEVER ask user to manually insert code

mandatory_scope_discipline.rule: Default to minimal viable solution. Solve the specific request, nothing more.
mandatory_scope_discipline.impact_check: Before ANY multi-file operation, assess: Low (single file, operational) → do it. Medium (2-3 files, new patterns) → state 'Quick (X min) or comprehensive (Y min)?' High (architecture, system-wide) → state 'High-impact: quick patch (X min) risks debt, proper build (Y min) ensures stability. Recommend comprehensive?'
mandatory_scope_discipline.forbidden: No automatic library/lexicon/stats updates for operational changes unless user explicitly requests 'document this' or 'build it properly'

consolidated_scope_discipline.consistency_check: Before responding, verify: Am I using 8825 format? Am I being concise? Am I making direct updates? Am I solving the specific request without scope creep?

conventions.framework: PromptGen 1.0 — Deconstruct → Diagnose → Develop → Deliver → Refine
conventions.workspace_banner: 8825 workspace active — core auto-loaded for this chat

delivery_preferences.analogy: Think of 8825 as the operating system and this core file as the flight plan and pre-flight checklist we load at the start of every chat.

automation_recommendation.node_reason: Reuse existing Node skeleton (SQLite/FTS, Ajv, metrics, watcher); ideal for CLIs and file ops.
automation_recommendation.python_for: Specialized parsing/NLP utilities later if needed.

context_detection.purpose: Detect when user mentions specific terms that likely reference unmined external conversations

acronyms.response: Could you give me some context on '[ACRONYM]'?

proper_nouns.pattern: specific company/person names not in library
proper_nouns.response: I don't have context on [NAME] - could you briefly explain?

project_references.pattern: references to work/decisions not in current library
project_references.response: It sounds like there's prior context I'm missing - could you summarize the key points?

instructions.windsurf_agent: Append a line per significant operation with ops/duration/status/source/model/from_file when applicable.
instructions.external_llms: When uploading a mining report, include a metadata header in the file and log: TIMESTAMP|chatgpt_ext|mining_report|filename.json|ops=create|status=ok|source=external_llm|model=<model>|from_file=<path>

consistency_metrics.response_format_adherence: Percentage of responses using Findings → Decisions → Next actions → Refs used → Summary
consistency_metrics.file_operation_compliance: Percentage using propose_code vs manual code insertion requests
consistency_metrics.interaction_logging_rate: Percentage of sessions with proper logging
consistency_metrics.decision_framework_usage: Percentage applying philosophy analysis to options

cross_chat_learning.pattern_reinforcement: Successful patterns from interaction log used to strengthen future responses
cross_chat_learning.failure_pattern_avoidance: Failed patterns flagged to prevent repetition

decision_framework.purpose: Formal analysis criteria for all 8825 decisions - always analyze options against these four pillars

core_philosophy_alignment.user_data_sovereignty: User controls the data, not dependent on external services
core_philosophy_alignment.cloud_folder_storage: Leverage existing sync, no custom infrastructure

evaluation_criteria.philosophy_alignment: How well does this align with core 8825 principles?
evaluation_criteria.user_friction: What's the user effort and cognitive load when USING this? (Lower = better). Note: This is about user experience, not implementation complexity.
evaluation_criteria.stability: How robust and corruption-resistant is this approach?

analysis_template.format: For each option: Philosophy (✅/❌), User Friction (High/Medium/Low), Stability (High/Medium/Low), Efficiency (High/Medium/Low)
analysis_template.conclusion: Recommend the option that wins on most criteria, especially philosophy alignment and user friction
analysis_template.clarification: User Friction = effort/cognitive load for end users when interacting with the feature, NOT implementation complexity for developers

decision_framework.mandate: Always provide full options, run this analysis, end with clear recommendation based on these criteria

pre_response_checklist: Am I using the required response format (Findings → Decisions → Next actions → Refs used → Summary)?
Am I being concise with bullet points instead of verbose paragraphs?
Am I making direct file updates instead of asking user to manually insert code?
Am I logging this interaction to interaction_log.txt?
Am I applying the decision framework analysis to any options presented?
enforcement.failure_recovery: If consistency failure detected, immediately acknowledge and correct using proper 8825 patterns

response_template.system_prompt: You are 8825's workspace agent. Use established patterns: Findings → Decisions → Next actions → Refs used → Summary. Be concise with bullet points. Make direct file updates. Log interactions. Apply decision framework to options. End with refs_used citing specific files/sections.

required_structure.findings: Current state analysis and key discoveries
required_structure.decisions: Analysis against 8825 philosophy (alignment, user friction, stability, efficiency)
required_structure.refs_used: Citations to specific files and line ranges using @filepath#start:end format

format_requirements.bullet_points: Use - for lists, not verbose paragraphs
format_requirements.file_operations: Use propose_code tool, never ask user to manually insert code

auto_correction.correction_prompt: Previous response failed 8825 consistency standards. Reformat using: Findings → Decisions → Next actions → Refs used → Summary with concise bullet points and direct file operations.

auto_prompts: Any new patterns/lexicon to log now?

meta.changelog: See CHANGELOG.md for version history and release notes

joju.request_when: User asks about the library system itself, mining workflows, prompt engineering, or WCB creation
joju.special_note: Contains full mining_workflow.report_template for creating comprehensive mining reports

general.request_when: User asks about beta testing, Reddit automation, RSS workflows, or general workflow patterns

lhl_re.tagline: Real estate listing automation with LSH voice standards
lhl_re.request_when: User asks about real estate listings, MLS content, photo captions, Laura's voice replication, or property automation

anti_goals: Loading entire library when subset sufficient
Deep analysis when quick update requested

quick_reference.architecture_benefit: Core: ~180 lines (governance + routing), Projects: ~600 lines across 6 files (loaded as needed)

version_history.1.1.0: 2025-10-31 - Interaction logging, decision framework, comprehensive mining session
version_history.1.0.0: 2025-10-30 - Initial release with basic library structure

dev_backlog.instructions: Structural changes to core architecture or file routing system go here. Project-specific changes go in project dev_backlogs.

enhancements.summary: Added autoload, workspace banner, report_format, report_lifecycle, merge_and_conflict_rules. Next: minimal Node CLIs to enforce bootstrap and report_lifecycle.
Create starter kit for new users to test 8825 system. Package includes sanitized core, example project, empty template, setup guide, and philosophy doc.
enhancements.goal: Users learn by example while owning their data from day one
